notes: Batons are regulated in France and may be owned, but carrying them in public is generally restricted without a valid reason.
Kubotans are generally legal in France as simple keychain tools when not clearly designed as weapons
Self-defence keychains are legal if they are ordinary items, but weaponised designs may be restricted.
Carrying a sword in public in France is restricted and generally requires a legitimate reason such as transport or events.
Tasers fall under regulated weapons in France and may be legal to own, but carrying them in public usually requires a valid reason.
Pepper spray is legal to possess within size limits but carrying it in public can be restricted
Switchblades are regulated in France and may be legal to own, but carrying them in public is restricted without justification.
Butterfly knives are regulated in France and may be owned, but public carry without valid reason is restricted.
Stun guns are regulated in France and may be legal to own, but carrying them in public is generally restricted.
Brass knuckles are prohibited weapons in France and cannot be legally owned or carried.
